title: Brand Voice Profile
description: Structured voice profile for a Kinetiks brand, written by /brand-voice and consumed by all content-producing skills. Validates the structured data that accompanies ./brand/{brand-name}/voice-profile.md.

brand_id.description: Which Kinetiks brand this profile belongs to.

brand_name.description: The brand or business name this voice profile belongs to.

last_updated.description: ISO 8601 date when this profile was last written or updated.

updated_by.description: The skill that last wrote this file, e.g. '/brand-voice'.

tone.description: The brand's tonal positioning across multiple spectrums.

spectrum.description: Array of tonal dimensions. Each dimension is a slider between two poles with the brand's position marked.

dimension.description: Name of this tonal dimension, e.g. 'Formality', 'Energy', 'Humor'.

left_pole.description: The label for the left end of the spectrum, e.g. 'Casual'.

right_pole.description: The label for the right end of the spectrum, e.g. 'Formal'.

position.description: Where the brand sits on this spectrum. 1 = fully left pole, 10 = fully right pole.

notes.description: Optional context about this dimension, e.g. 'More formal in sales pages, casual in emails'.

summary.description: A one-sentence natural-language summary of the overall tone, e.g. 'Conversational but sharp, with dry humor and zero fluff.'

vocabulary.description: Words and phrases the brand embraces or avoids.

preferred.description: Words, phrases, or patterns the brand actively uses.

context.description: When or how to use this term, e.g. 'Use in CTAs' or 'Only in casual contexts'.

avoid.description: Words, phrases, or patterns the brand never uses.

term.description: The word or phrase to avoid.

reason.description: Why this term is avoided, e.g. 'Sounds corporate' or 'Overused in our niche'.

alternative.description: What to use instead, if applicable.

personality_traits.description: 3-7 adjectives or short phrases that define the brand's personality. Ordered by prominence.

examples.description: Concrete examples of on-brand and off-brand writing to calibrate content-producing skills.

on_brand.description: Samples of writing that nail this brand's voice. At least 2 recommended.

source.description: Where this came from, e.g. 'Homepage hero', 'Email subject line', 'User-provided'.

off_brand.description: Samples of writing that violate this brand's voice. Useful for calibration.

source.description: Where this came from or was fabricated as an anti-example.

platform_adaptations.description: How the voice shifts across platforms. Keys are platform names, values describe adaptations.

tone_shift.description: How the tone changes on this platform, e.g. 'More casual, shorter sentences'.

format_preferences.description: Structural preferences, e.g. 'Use line breaks between every sentence on LinkedIn'.

length.description: Typical content length, e.g. 'Under 280 chars' or '800-1200 words'.

audience_awareness.description: How voice-aware the brand is of its audience's sophistication and expectations.

sophistication_level.description: How technical or experienced the primary audience is.

jargon_tolerance.description: How much industry jargon the audience expects or tolerates.

reading_level.description: Target reading level, e.g. '8th grade' or 'professional'.

notes.description: Additional context about audience expectations around voice.

signature_phrases.description: Recurring phrases, taglines, or verbal tics that make the brand recognizable. These can be woven into content when natural.

usage.description: When to use this, e.g. 'Email sign-offs', 'Opening hooks', 'Everywhere'.
